arts.ttl: Marseille and Eintracht Frankfurt reach €26m agreement for Elye Wahi
arts.sum: Marseille centre-forward Elye Wahi (22) is edging closer to joining Eintracht Frankfurt as both clubs have reportedly reached an agreement, L’Equipe understands. The France U21 international is set to leave…

arts.ttl: Eintracht Frankfurt increase offer for Elye Wahi as agreement with Marseille nears
arts.sum: Elye Wahi (22) is nearing a move away from Olympique de Marseille, just six months after joining the club, according to a report from Fabrizio Romano. Wahi joined OM from RC…

arts.ttl: Marseille turn down first Frankfurt bid for potential Marmoush replacement
arts.sum: According to Sky Germany, Eintracht Frankfurt's first bid for Elye Wahi has been rejected by Olympique Marseille.

arts.ttl: 'Some things': Omar Marmoush already knows which player he now wants to learn from after signing for Man City
arts.sum: Omar Marmoush is a man with a plan 🎯

arts.ttl: Omar Marmoush says goodbye to Eintracht Frankfurt after Manchester City move confirmed
arts.sum: Fare thee well, Omar Marmoush: the Eintracht Frankfurt forward, who lit up the Bundesliga with his insatiable thirst for goals and assists, is on his way to Manchester City after bidding an emotional auf Wiedersehen at Deutsche Bank Park. The Egyptian magician certainly left a mark on his time in Germany's top flight and, in particular, with Eintracht, for whom his all-star performances have helped the team from Mainhatten to challenge at the top of the standings this term .

arts.ttl: The surprise, late rise of Man City newcomer Omar Marmoush
arts.sum: It's been a circuitous route to the top for Omar Marmoush but, at the age of 25, he has finally arrived after a €60m transfer to Manchester City.
In praise of Omar Marmoush following his record-breaking time in Frankfurt.

arts.ttl: Manchester City seal Marmoush deal, taking January spend to £122.5m
arts.sum: Forward costs initial £59.1m and is third January buy ‘This is a day I will never forget,’ says Egypt international Manchester City have confirmed the signing of Omar Marmoush for €70m (£59.1m) plus €5m in add-ons from Eintracht Frankfurt, the forward stating delight at joining a “winning environment and culture”. The Egyptian, who has a four-and-a-half-year contract, is the Premier League champions’ third acquisition of the window. He follows the defenders Vitor Reis, bought from Palmeiras for £29.6m, and Abdukodir Khusanov, signed for £33.8m from Lens, taking City’s spend to £122.5m. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Why Hugo Ekitiké can replace Omar Marmoush‘s goals at Eintracht Frankfurt
arts.sum: Matchday 18 saw Frankfurt host Borussia Dortmund, with Ekitiké leading the line without Marmoush by his side for the first time this season, and he took only 18 minutes to prove that the Egyptian won’t be taking all the goals with him when he departs. Having scored 11 goals in 28 games in the 2021/22 season for Reims at the age of 19, Ekitiké caught the eye of numerous clubs across Europe, and eventually chose to stay in Ligue 1 with Paris Saint-Germain.

arts.ttl: Preview: Eintracht Frankfurt vs. Ferencvaros - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Thursday's Europa League clash between Eintracht Frankfurt and Ferencvaros, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.
